title: KPIT founder Ravi Pandit passes away
summary: KPIT Technologies founder and chairman S B Ravi Pandit passed away on May 8, 2026. Pandit established KPIT in 1990. He guided the company into a global leader in automotive software, electrification, and mobility solutions. KPIT partnered with major automotive firms on advanced technologies.

title: Sony forecasts higher games profit but lower sales amid memory price surge
summary: Sony anticipates a profit jump of 11% to 1.6 trillion yen by March 2027. The company expects lower sales in its gaming division but higher profits due to strong software releases. Sony will also buy back shares worth up to 500 billion yen. Investors are watching chip prices and supply chain issues.

title: Rave files antitrust lawsuit against Apple over removal of video-sharing app
summary: Rave, a video sharing app, has sued Apple in a US federal court. Rave claims Apple removed its app from the App Store to promote its own SharePlay feature. The company seeks significant damages and reinstatement. Rave has also filed similar lawsuits in other countries.

title: Tech is turning increasingly to religion in a quest to create ethical AI
summary: Tech companies are seeking guidance from faith leaders to infuse morality into AI development, a shift from Silicon Valley's past skepticism. The inaugural "Faith-AI Covenant" roundtable brought together religious representatives and AI firms to discuss ethical principles.

title: Cloudflare to cut about 20% workforce as AI adoption reshapes operations
summary: Cloudflare announced significant layoffs, cutting approximately 20% of its workforce, impacting over 1,100 global employees. This restructuring is driven by the company's rapid adoption of artificial intelligence tools and a shift towards an 'agentic AI-first operating model.' Despite stronger first

title: Apple, Meta warn Canadian bill could force them to weaken encryption
summary: Apple and Meta are opposing Canada's Bill C-22, arguing it could force them to break device and service encryption. Law enforcement claims the bill aids investigations, but tech companies warn it risks weakening user security by potentially requiring backdoors or government spyware.
